dimensionDescriptions.network_beaconing: Explain whether periodicity, low-and-slow transfer shape, and callback regularity indicate beaconing; justify why timing patterns are operationally meaningful rather than incidental.
dimensionDescriptions.network_exfiltration: Explain whether observed flow volume, outbound/inbound asymmetry, staging timing, and destination characteristics support or weaken an exfiltration interpretation.
dimensionDescriptions.benign_traffic_explanations: Explain plausible non-malicious causes (for example software updates, backups, SaaS synchronization, or admin tooling) and justify why they do or do not fit the observed sequence.
dimensionDescriptions.dns_patterns: Explain DNS evidence such as TTL behavior, randomized or algorithmic subdomain patterns, and query cadence, and justify what these characteristics imply.
dimensionDescriptions.tls_metadata: Explain how TLS metadata (for example SNI, certificate patterns, and JA3-style client fingerprint context) supports, contradicts, or fails to discriminate between hypotheses.
dimensionDescriptions.proxy_http_indicators: Explain what proxy/HTTP details (user-agent shifts, status code patterns, request methods, byte counts, and transfer windows) reveal about likely intent and activity type.
dimensionDescriptions.endpoint_to_network_linkage: Explain how endpoint process/user/service context links to network behavior, including parent-child lineage, execution context, and whether behavior aligns with expected software function.
dimensionDescriptions.investigation_pivot: Explain the single highest-value next check (or top two), why it reduces uncertainty fastest, and what specific outcome would change your conclusion.
dimensionDescriptions.containment_tradeoff: Explain containment choice tradeoffs, including disruption risk, visibility loss, evidence preservation impact, and how to mitigate downside.
dimensionDescriptions.risk_reasoning: Explain how selected risk level follows from likely impact, exposure, and current evidence quality rather than isolated indicators.
dimensionDescriptions.confidence_reasoning: Explain how confidence level is justified by corroboration strength, competing interpretations, and telemetry limitations.
dimensionDescriptions.evidence_linkage: Explain which concrete artifacts support each major claim and clearly separate direct evidence from inference.
dimensionDescriptions.hypothesis_alignment: Explain how your final hypothesis aligns with your decision path and why alternatives are less defensible at this stage.

reportSections.title: Executive Summary
reportSections.template: State your current network finding (beaconing, exfiltration, benign, or blended), business-relevant risk statement, and immediate recommendation in concise decision language.

reportSections.title: Traffic Characterization
reportSections.template: Describe the dominant traffic pattern and why it matters: periodicity, burst behavior, directionality, destination type, and whether it is more consistent with beaconing, exfiltration, or sanctioned automation.

reportSections.title: Key Evidence (3–6 artifacts)
reportSections.template: List 3–6 high-value artifacts (for example DNS cadence, TLS metadata, proxy byte counts, endpoint lineage, change ticket timing) and state exactly what each artifact supports or weakens.

reportSections.title: Hypothesis and Alternatives
reportSections.template: Defend your leading hypothesis and at least one alternative interpretation, then explain why your chosen interpretation is currently the most defensible.

reportSections.title: Confidence and Uncertainty
reportSections.template: State confidence level, justify why it is not higher or lower, and identify specific unknowns or telemetry limits that could materially change the assessment.

reportSections.title: Investigation Pivot Plan
reportSections.template: Name the top 1–2 next checks that reduce uncertainty fastest and define the discriminating outcomes that would confirm, weaken, or reverse your conclusion.

reportSections.title: Containment Recommendation and Tradeoffs
reportSections.template: Recommend immediate containment posture, then explicitly state visibility/evidence-preservation loss and business impact, plus compensating controls for those downsides.

reportSections.title: Professional Conclusion (next 24–72 hours)
reportSections.template: Provide an actionable 24–72 hour plan with ownership, telemetry to monitor, and decision triggers for escalation, containment adjustment, or de-escalation.

gapRules.prompt: Specify the single highest-value next check and the exact discriminating result you expect that would separate beaconing, exfiltration, and benign automation interpretations.
Link at least one process/user/service context detail to the network behavior and explain why that linkage strengthens or weakens your hypothesis.
State one concrete downside of your containment choice (visibility, evidence preservation, or business disruption) and one compensating control to reduce that downside.
Justify your confidence level explicitly by tying evidence strength and unresolved telemetry limits to why confidence is low, medium, or high.
